features.featureTitle: Apply a professional enterprise visual theme across the entire application: deep navy/slate primary palette with teal/cyan accents, clean sans-serif typography (Inter or similar), card-based layouts with subtle shadows, status-color coding (yellow=Submitted, blue=Approved, green=Completed, red=Rejected, orange=In Fulfillment), and a collapsible left sidebar navigation. Ensure the design is responsive and mobile-friendly.

Implement a Login Page with a company-branded sign-in form (email + password fields), an Internet Identity login button, and an MFA indicator badge. The page must show a role selector (Employee, Manager, IT Admin, Finance Admin) to simulate RBAC personas for demo purposes.

Create a main Dashboard page showing: (1) a summary row of KPI cards — Total Requests, Pending Approvals, Completed This Month, SLA Breaches; (2) a recent requests table with columns for Request ID, Type, Status badge, Submitted Date, and SLA countdown; (3) a requests-by-status donut chart; (4) a requests-by-category bar chart. The dashboard must adapt its visible actions based on the logged-in role.

Build a Create Request page with a multi-step wizard (3 steps max): Step 1 — select request type (System Access, Physical Access, Equipment, Software Licenses) with icon cards; Step 2 — fill request details form (sub-category, justification text, priority selector, cost estimate, document attachment field); Step 3 — AI Pre-Submission Analysis panel showing duplicate access warnings, missing info alerts, suggested access level, and recommended approval chain, with a 'Confirm & Submit' button. After submit, save the request to the backend.

Build a Request Detail Page showing: full request metadata (type, category, status, priority, cost, justification), an approval chain timeline (showing each approver node, their status, and timestamp), a status progress stepper (Submitted → Under Review → Approved → In Fulfillment → Completed), a SLA countdown timer, AI risk flags panel (e.g., 'Warning: user already has admin access in 3 systems'), and an activity/audit log feed at the bottom.

Build an Approvals Screen (visible to Manager, IT Admin, Finance Admin roles) listing all requests pending their approval. Each row shows: requester name, request type, risk level badge, cost, and action buttons (Approve, Reject, Request More Info). Implement intelligent approval routing logic in the backend: low-risk software requests are auto-approved; high-risk requests require Manager + IT Security; requests over $1,000 require Finance approval. Approving or rejecting a request updates its status and appends an audit log entry.

Build an AI Chatbot Assistant panel (floating button on all pages that expands to a sidebar chat). The chatbot accepts natural language input, simulates NLP parsing to extract structured fields (category, priority, quantity, reason), displays the parsed request summary, and offers a one-click 'Create This Request' button that pre-fills the Create Request form. Include example prompt suggestions like 'I need access to CRM for a marketing campaign' and 'Need laptop urgently for new joiner tomorrow'.

Build a Reports & Export page (Admin/Management role only) with: (1) a filter bar for date range, department, and request type; (2) a summary statistics section with charts (requests over time line chart, approval rate pie chart, SLA compliance bar chart); (3) export buttons for CSV download and a printable PDF summary view; (4) a department-wise breakdown table; (5) an audit trail log table with search and pagination. Generate report data from the backend's stored requests and audit logs.

Build an Admin Configuration page with tabs for: (1) User Management — list users, assign roles, set manager delegates; (2) SLA Rules — configure SLA timelines per request category; (3) Approval Chain Policies — define which roles approve which request types and risk levels; (4) System Notifications — toggle in-app notification preferences. All configuration changes must persist to the backend.

Implement an in-app notification system: a bell icon in the top navigation bar shows unread notification count badge. Clicking opens a notification dropdown listing alerts such as 'Your request #123 has been approved', 'SLA breach warning for request #456', and 'New request pending your approval'. Notifications are fetched from the backend and can be marked as read.
